original_abstract: Risk is the one of basic and inevitable characteristics of every human activity. In connection with business activities, it is the factor that is not possible to entirely eliminate. The fact that it is possible only to minimize a risk, respectively to minimize its negative consequences at certain conditions fulfilment is one o the risk specifications.The problem of risk classification is seriously wide. In scientific literature, various categorisations of risk are described and discussed while the categorisation is usually based on the purpose of presented classification.Risk categorisation represents the file of a risk into the one of existing categories constructed on the basis of parameters of classified risks. In the work, the authors summarize the theoretical backgrounds of risk categorisation, and it is especially in consequences of risk connected with business activities.The objective of the article is to provide the synthesis of theoretical categorisations of risk that would be contributing to the discipline of risk management as to the scientific discipline.
humanized_abstract: Risk is one of the basic and inalienable attributes of any human activity. In business, it can not be completely eliminated. Therefore, it can only be reduced to a minimum, while its harmful effects are minimized under certain conditions. The risk classification is a very wide field. In scientific literature, a number of risk classifications are presented and discussed and the purpose of the classification is always reflected in the method of risk classification. Risk categorisation means to classify the file of a risk into one of the pre-defined categories based on the parameters of the respective risk. In the article, the authors present a summarised review of the theoretical aspects of risk categorisation and especially the consequences of risks connected with business. The aim of the article is to give an overview of the theoretical risk classifications that contribute to the Risk Management discipline as a scientific discipline.

Risk categorisation means to classify the file of a risk into one of the pre-defined categories based on the parameters of the respective risk. In the article, the authors present a summarised review of the theoretical aspects of risk categorisation and especially the consequences of risks connected with business. The aim of the article is to give an overview of the theoretical risk classifications that contribute to the Risk Management discipline as a scientific discipline.
